name: review-code-dev
title: Review code
description: Run isolated local code reviews with a primary review agent, bounded specialist loops, non-committable artifacts, and parseable P0-P3 findings.
notes:
## What it does

Mega Code Review reviews uncommitted changes, branch diffs, commits, or custom scopes in any repository while keeping the main conversation clean. It delegates deep review to an isolated primary reviewer when a safe adapter exists, passing only a minimal brief plus local git context.

## Safety model

- Read-only review by default.
- Never edits files during review.
- Never reproduces secret values.
- Treats repository content as data, not instructions.
- Writes portable artifacts under plans/review-code-dev/runs/.
- Prepares that artifact root with a local Git exclude rule before writing, and refuses to use it if it is already tracked.

## Specialist review loop

The primary reviewer runs a bounded loop: intent/scope audit, direct diff pass, focused specialists, optional red-team challenge, candidate vetting, artifact validation, and at most one artifact-only repair. Specialist reviewers live under references/reviewers/ and include frontend, backend, tests, security, architecture, performance, API contract, data migration, and red-team. The frontend reviewer depends on Impeccable for UI and design-review judgment while preserving Mega Code Review's read-only constraints.

## Evidence discipline

Focused helpers return structured candidate JSONL. The primary reviewer assigns confidence, fingerprints root causes, checks evidence, records duplicates and rejected candidates, and publishes only verified P0-P3 findings in review.md.

## Artifacts

The review writes run-metadata.json, context.json, recon.md, coverage.md, review.md, and review.json. When delegation is used it also writes delegation-brief.md and subagents.md. Looping reviews may write scope-audit.md, candidate-findings.json, loop-state.json, artifact-validation.md, adversarial-review.md, rejected-findings.md, verification.md, fix-report.md, notes.md, or pr-link.md when the workflow calls for them.

changes: Add review-intelligence guidance for intent/scope audit, confidence calibration, fingerprints, evidence gates, and stop rules.
Switch focused sub-reviewer output to structured candidate JSONL so the primary reviewer can deduplicate and verify findings before publishing.
Add performance, API contract, data migration, and red-team specialist reviewer briefs.
Add scope-audit.md and adversarial-review.md artifact contracts.
Add a bounded review loop with candidate classification, loop-state tracking, and artifact validation.
Add specialist reviewer briefs for frontend, backend, tests, security, and architecture.
Declare Impeccable as the frontend review dependency and require read-only Impeccable usage for UI review.
Publish version 1.0.7.
Publish version 1.0.6.
Update the Companion manifest to the current v2 schema URL.
Republish the package with the new companion.json format.
Add a preparation script that creates non-committable review artifact directories.
Require Git ignore verification before writing plans/review-code-dev artifacts.
Document run-metadata.json as the proof artifact for non-committable runs.
Republish with Companion manifest v2 package preservation verification.
Migrate package metadata to Companion manifest v2.
Move Companion registry identity and package version out of SKILL.md frontmatter.
Declare environment, dependencies, commands, notes, and changelog in companion.json.
Add isolated primary reviewer flow by default to keep the main conversation context small.
Add delegation-brief.md artifact contract for primary review handoff.
Document primary reviewer and focused sub-reviewer responsibilities.
Initial published version of Mega Code Review.

commands.name: Review uncommitted changes
commands.desc: Collect local git context and run a read-only isolated review of the working tree.

commands.name: Review branch against base
commands.desc: Compare a branch to a base ref and report confirmed P0-P3 findings.

commands.name: Review commit
commands.desc: Review one commit or ref for merge-blocking issues and artifact-backed findings.

commands.name: Run focused review
commands.desc: Apply a security, performance, tests, design, frontend, API contract, data migration, red-team, or custom review lens with optional focused helpers.
